perturbation.original_text: The coaching service provided by Mentor is worth a maximum fee of EUR 5,000 (VAT excluded) per Participant coached , that will be remunerated to Mentor at the end of the programme, after the submission and approval of the final summary report of the coaching relationships. Payment shall be made within 30 days from the receipt of invoice, and following the invoice verification by Organiser. No deductions w ill be made from the gross fees paid to mentor who shall be solely responsible for ensuring that all and any Government taxes and other deductions are paid by him/her.
perturbation.changed_text: The coaching service provided by Mentor will be remunerated at the end of the programme, after the submission and approval of the final summary report of the coaching relationships. Payment shall be made following the invoice verification by Organiser. No deductions w ill be made from the fees paid to mentor.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific fee amount (EUR 5,000) for the coaching service, the contract becomes ambiguous about the mentor's compensation. The text still promises remuneration, but the amount is undefined, which creates an in-text contradiction because it implies payment without a defined value. In addition, removing the mention of VAT exclusion and the clause stating that the mentor is responsible for his/her taxes opens the possibility for later disputes with the organiser. Removing `gross` before `fees` is an additional change that adds more amiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment The Organiser will reimburse Mentor the travel arrangements and accommodation for the Acceleration Bootcamp in Tallinn in 2017 (a maximum of EUR 800) and the closing bootcamp in 2018 (a maximum of EUR 700) . The coaching service provided by Mentor will be remunerated at the end of the programme, after the submission and approval of the final summary report of the coaching relationships. Payment shall be made following the invoice verification by Organiser. No deductions w ill be made from the fees paid to mentor.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a maximum fee of EUR 5,000 for the coaching service. The changed text removes this specific amount, creating ambiguity regarding the payment amount. The contradicted text includes the changed text, and still references remuneration without defining a value. This creates an in-text contradiction because it implies payment without a defined value, making it impossible to enforce the payment terms as originally intended. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Monthly v irtual interactions need to be planned by Mentor, to ensure a continuous acceleration of the Participants Ideas ´ towards business maturity. A mid -term review and survey will be required by April 2018, to evaluate progress made and ensure that the recommend ations of the coaching plan are currently implemented by Participants.
perturbation.changed_text: Virtual interactions need to be planned by Mentor, to ensure a continuous acceleration of the Participants Ideas ´ towards business maturity. A review may be required to evaluate progress made.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'monthly' requirement for virtual interactions and making the review optional ('may be required' instead of 'will be required'), the contract becomes weaker and more ambiguous about the mentor's responsibilities. The section still mentions 'virtual interactions' and 'review' but their frequency and necessity are undefined, creating an in-text contradiction with the original intent of regular monitoring and evaluation. The omission of the April 2018 deadline adds to the uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Procedure and Terms of Participation The Copernicus Accelerator programme is comprised of different phases, for a total coaching support of approximately one year , aimed at helping Participants´ Ideas achieving measurable results. When submitting the request to become Mentor, Mentor needs to specify in which fields he/she can support Participants, in order to allow a suitable matchmaking with the requests and needs communicated by Participants. When a Mentor is requested by a Participant of the programme, he/she can access the Participant's profile and examine the submitted idea, in order to decide whether to confirm the request or not. Mentors can only advise up to two participants. Mentors will meet the Participants at the Accelerator Bootcamp , that will take place in line with the Awards Ceremony of the Copernicus Masters , the European Satellite Navigation Competition (ESNC) , and the accompanying Satellite Masters Conference from 6 - 8 November 2017 in Tallinn , Estonia . Mentor´s participation to this event is mandatory, as it will set forth the official start of their relationship: Mentor and Participants will begin developing a coaching plan together, that will include objectives and KPIs of their affiliation. Virtual interactions need to be planned by Mentor, to ensure a continuous acceleration of the Participants Ideas ´ towards business maturity. A review may be required to evaluate progress made. In case of a non -positive matching or non-compliance to his/her assigned duties (having monthly interactions with the mentees, keeping contacts with the Copernicus Accelerator team , promptly informing the organizers about any relevant issue connected to the coaching relationship) , Mentor may be replaced by a more suitable candidate for the Participants´ needs. Mentor will actively support Participants until Ju ly 201 8, and will then submit a final report together, detailing the outcome of the coaching experience. The Organiser will continuously monitor all relationships, in order to assist both parties and guarantee a positive outcome of the programme. Mentor ne eds to deliver all requested reports and schedule all requested meetings. The Organiser will provide Mentor with templates for the documents to be produced. Mentors and Participants will meet again at the closing bootcamp, held in October/November 2018, th at will official seal the closing of their mentoring relationship. At the end of the coaching service, Mentor will fill in a questionnaire on the coaching service and the overall experience and will also be subject to a specific questionnaire compiled by Participants.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified 'monthly virtual interactions' and a mandatory mid-term review by April 2018. The changed text removes the 'monthly' requirement and makes the review optional ('may be required'), directly contradicting the 'Procedure and Terms of Participation' section. The contradicted text states 'having monthly interactions with the mentees' as a duty, and 'Mentor needs to deliver all requested reports and schedule all requested meetings' which are both undermined by the modified text's removal of the 'monthly' requirement and making the review optional, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as the mentor's obligations become significantly less defined and enforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Mentor agrees to segregate all Confidential Information relating to this agreement from Confidential Information of others to avoid commingling.
perturbation.changed_text: Mentor agrees to use his best efforts to segregate Confidential Information relating to this agreement from Confidential Information of others.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'segregate' to 'use his best efforts to segregate', the contract weakens the obligation on the mentor to protect Confidential Information. It introduces a subjective standard ('best efforts') instead of a strict requirement. The word 'commingling' is removed. This change introduces an in-text contradiction because elsewhere, the contract stipulates a high degree of care for Confidential Information, implying a more rigorous standard than 'best efforts'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non -Disclosure of Confidential Information / Obligations of Mentor Mentor shall not use any Co nfidential Information for any purpose except to review, assess and help develop the Participants´ Ideas. Mentor shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party or to Mentor ’s employees and/or employer without the prior written consent o f the Participants. Mentor shall require his/her 5 employees who will have access to Confidential Information to commit to a non -disclosure agreement that protects the Confidential Information to at least the same degree as this Agreement. Mentor shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of, and avoid any unauthorised disclosure or use of Confidential Information. Such measures shall include the highest degree of care that Mentor utilises to protect Mentor ’s own confidential information of a similar nature, but no less than reasonable care. Notwithstanding Mentor ’s right to assess and rate the Ideas of the Participants, Mentor shall not use the Confidential information for his/her own or third parties purposes and shall not file for any intel lectual property right protection for the Confidential Information or parts of it. Mentor shall notify Organiser immediately in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of any Confidential Information that may come to Mentor ’s attention. Mentor agrees to use his best efforts to segregate Confidential Information relating to this agreement from Confidential Information of others.
perturbation.justification: The original text required Mentor to 'segregate' Confidential Information. The changed text weakens this to 'use his best efforts to segregate'. The contradicted text imposes a high standard of care for Confidential Information, including 'the highest degree of care that Mentor utilises to protect Mentor’s own confidential information of a similar nature, but no less than reasonable care'. Changing 'segregate' to 'use his best efforts to segregate' and removing 'commingling' directly contradicts the requirement to take 'all reasonable measures' and employ 'the highest degree of care', as 'best efforts' is a subjective and potentially lower standard than the stipulated 'highest degree of care', thus creating a strong in-text contradiction based on the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: At Organiser ’s first request, Mentor shall: (a) discontinue all use of Confiden tial Information; (b) return to Organiser and/or Participants all materials furnished by Organiser and/or Participants that contain Confidential Information; (c) destroy any copy and all materials produced by and under control of Organiser and/or Partici pants that contain Confidential Information; (d) erase and/or destroy any Confidential Information contained in computer memory or data storage apparatus of, under control of or used by Mentor; (e) remove the Confidential Information from any software or data base of, under control of/or used by Mentor that incorporates or uses the Confidential Information in whole or in part; and (f) warrant in writing to Organiser, within ten (10) days after Organiser ’s request, that Mentor has taken all actions set ou t under (a) through (e) in this Clause 4.
perturbation.changed_text: At Organiser ’s request, Mentor shall: (a) discontinue all use of Confiden tial Information; (b) return to Organiser and/or Participants all materials furnished by Organiser and/or Participants that contain Confidential Information; (c) destroy any copy and all materials produced by and under control of Organiser and/or Partici pants that contain Confidential Information; (d) erase and/or destroy any Confidential Information contained in computer memory or data storage apparatus of, under control of or used by Mentor; (e) remove the Confidential Information from any software or data base of, under control of/or used by Mentor that incorporates or uses the Confidential Information in whole or in part.
perturbation.explanation: By removing section (f), the mentor is no longer explicitly required to provide written confirmation of compliance with the data destruction and return requests. The 'first' request is also removed. This creates ambiguity and weakens the enforcement mechanism for ensuring that Confidential Information is properly handled after the agreement. This change introduces an in-text contradiction to the proprietary rights clause, because there is no longer written proof that these actions were carried out.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Proprietary Rights, Limited Right to Use Any and all proprietary rights, including but not limited to rights to and in inventions, patent rights, utility models, copyrights, trademarks and trade secrets, in and to any Confidential Information shall be and remain with the Participants respectively, and Mentor shall not have any right, license, title or interest in or to any Confidential Information, except the limited right to review, assess and he lp develop such Confidential Information in connection with the Copernicus Accelerator 201 7.
perturbation.justification: The removal of section (f) in the Changed Text eliminates the Mentor's obligation to provide written confirmation that they have complied with the data destruction and return requirements. This directly contradicts the Proprietary Rights clause in the Contradicted Text, as there is no longer a guaranteed mechanism to ensure that the Participants' proprietary rights in the Confidential Information are protected through verifiable action. The explanation supports this since it mentions that there is no longer written proof that these actions were carried out which would cause an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Mentor is aware that an unauthorised disclosure of the Ideas and of the assessment and development of the Ideas and of any Confidential Information may lead to serious damage to Participant s and the Organiser of the Copernicus Accelerator 201 7.
perturbation.changed_text: Mentor is aware that an unauthorised disclosure of Confidential Information may lead to damage to Participant s and the Organiser of the Copernicus Accelerator 201 7.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'of the Ideas and of the assessment and development of the Ideas', the clause is narrowed to only cover damage due to unauthorised disclosure of 'Confidential Information'. This implicitly suggests that actions related to the ideas that are not confidential are excluded from the clause. This creates an in-text contradiction with other sections that imply a broader scope of liability for the mentor regarding the entire innovation process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non -Disclosure of Confidential Information / Obligations of Mentor Mentor shall not use any Co nfidential Information for any purpose except to review, assess and help develop the Participants´ Ideas. Mentor shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party or to Mentor ’s employees and/or employer without the prior written consent o f the Participants. Mentor shall require his/her 5 employees who will have access to Confidential Information to commit to a non -disclosure agreement that protects the Confidential Information to at least the same degree as this Agreement. Mentor shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of, and avoid any unauthorised disclosure or use of Confidential Information. Such measures shall include the highest degree of care that Mentor utilises to protect Mentor ’s own confidential information of a similar nature, but no less than reasonable care. Notwithstanding Mentor ’s right to assess and rate the Ideas of the Participants, Mentor shall not use the Confidential information for his/her own or third parties purposes and shall not file for any intel lectual property right protection for the Confidential Information or parts of it. Mentor shall notify Organiser immediately in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of any Confidential Information that may come to Mentor ’s attention. Mentor agrees to use his best efforts to segregate Confidential Information relating to this agreement from Confidential Information of others.
perturbation.justification: The original text covered unauthorised disclosure of 'Ideas and assessment and development of Ideas and Confidential Information'. The changed text narrows this to only 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text states that the Mentor shall use Confidential Information to review, assess and help develop the Participants' Ideas and shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of, and avoid any unauthorised disclosure or use of Confidential Information. By removing 'Ideas and assessment and development of Ideas' from the initial clause, the changed text now implies that unauthorised disclosure of ideas that are not confidential is not covered under the same liability as before, which contradicts the broader scope of the mentor's obligations regarding the ideas presented in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Professionals w ho pursue becoming mentors need to apply via the dedicated platform , inserting all required details, which can be edited anytime by Mentor. Their request ne eds to be validated by the Organiser and the European Commission, who can refuse participation in case of non -eligibility or conflict of interest. After verification of all details entered, the Organiser will notify the Mentor about the outcome of his/her submission. Even after validation, the Organiser cannot guarantee that there will be an actual match between Mentor and Participants.
perturbation.changed_text: Professionals w ho pursue becoming mentors need to apply via the dedicated platform , inserting all required details. Their request ne eds to be validated by the Organiser, who can refuse participation in case of non -eligibility or conflict of interest. After verification of all details entered, the Organiser will notify the Mentor about the outcome of his/her submission.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to the 'European Commission' having a role in validating mentor requests, the agreement seems to grant the Organiser sole discretion. This creates an in-text contradiction because the preamble states that the Copernicus Accelerator is a 'European Commission programme', implying the Commission has oversight. Also removing the last sentance about the guarantee of actual match creates additional amiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Procedure and Terms of Participation The Copernicus Accelerator programme is comprised of different phases, for a total coaching support of approximately one year , aimed at helping Participants´ Ideas achieving measurable results. When submitting the request to become Mentor, Mentor needs to specify in which fields he/she can support Participants, in order to allow a suitable matchmaking with the requests and needs communicated by Participants. When a Mentor is requested by a Participant of the programme, he/she can access the Participant's profile and examine the submitted idea, in order to decide whether to confirm the request or not. Mentors can only advise up to two participants. Mentors will meet the Participants at the Accelerator Bootcamp , that will take place in line with the Awards Ceremony of the Copernicus Masters , the European Satellite Navigation Competition (ESNC) , and the accompanying Satellite Masters Conference from 6 - 8 November 2017 in Tallinn , Estonia . Mentor´s participation to this event is mandatory, as it will set forth the official start of their relationship: Mentor and Participants will begin developing a coaching plan together, that will include objectives and KPIs of their affiliation. Virtual interactions need to be planned by Mentor, to ensure a continuous acceleration of the Participants Ideas ´ towards business maturity. A review may be required to evaluate progress made. In case of a non -positive matching or non-compliance to his/her assigned duties (having monthly interactions with the mentees, keeping contacts with the Copernicus Accelerator team , promptly informing the organizers about any relevant issue connected to the coaching relationship) , Mentor may be replaced by a more suitable candidate for the Participants´ needs. Mentor will actively support Participants until Ju ly 201 8, and will then submit a final report together, detailing the outcome of the coaching experience. The Organiser will continuously monitor all relationships, in order to assist both parties and guarantee a positive outcome of the programme. Mentor ne eds to deliver all requested reports and schedule all requested meetings. The Organiser will provide Mentor with templates for the documents to be produced. Mentors and Participants will meet again at the closing bootcamp, held in October/November 2018, th at will official seal the closing of their mentoring relationship. At the end of the coaching service, Mentor will fill in a questionnaire on the coaching service and the overall experience and will also be subject to a specific questionnaire compiled by Participants.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that both the 'Organiser and the European Commission' have a role in validating mentor requests. The changed text removes the 'European Commission', granting the Organiser sole discretion. The 'Contradicted Text' describes the 'Copernicus Accelerator programme' and alludes to various procedures associated with the program, including mentorship. Removing the European Commission's oversight, in the Changed Text contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which implicitly states that the European Commission has oversight and is part of the Copernicus Accelerator Program. The Contradicted Text refers to a series of procedures which assumes the involvement of the European Commission.
